query: US Treasury market outlook May 2026, bond yields, trade deal negotiations impact on rates
answer:
Excellent — the sub-agent found comprehensive data from local research files. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# US Treasury Market Outlook — May 2026

## 1. Current Treasury Yields (May 5–6, 2026)

The Treasury yield curve is positively sloped — a return to normality after the prolonged 2024–2025 inversion.

| Maturity | Yield (May 5 close) | Intraday May 6 | Change (1-month) |
|----------|---------------------|-----------------|-------------------|
| 3-Month T-Bill | 3.69% | — | — |
| 2-Year | 3.93% | 3.867% | +9 bps |
| 10-Year | 4.43% | 4.347% | +13 bps |
| 30-Year | 4.98% | ~4.95% | +7 bps |

- **10Y–2Y spread:** +50 bps (positively sloped, normalized)
- **10-Year TIPS real yield:** 1.95%
- **10-Year breakeven inflation:** ~2.50%
- The 10-year briefly touched **4.45%** on May 4 — its highest since late March. The 30-year briefly pierced **5.02%** before settling.
- On May 6, yields dropped sharply intraday (10Y down ~8 bps) on news of Iran ceasefire/deal talks.

### Yield Trajectory (March → May 2026)
Yields have risen significantly since early March, driven by the Iran war energy shock and inflation reacceleration:
- **2-Year:** 3.47% → 3.93% (+46 bps)
- **10-Year:** 4.05% → 4.43% (+38 bps)

## 2. Key Drivers of Treasury Market Dynamics

### A. Iran War and Energy Shock (Dominant Driver)
The US-Israel strikes on Iran ("Operation Midnight Hammer," Feb 28, 2026) and the resulting closure of the Strait of Hormuz have been the single largest market driver:
- **Brent crude:** surged from ~$70–75/bbl pre-war to a peak above **$125/bbl**; currently ~$110–114/bbl
- **US gasoline:** $2.98 → $4.45/gal (+49%)
- The energy shock directly pushed Q1 2026 PCE inflation to **+4.5% annualized** (core: +4.3%)
- On **May 6**, emerging Iran deal talks via Pakistan mediation caused oil to drop and yields to fall intraday, demonstrating the tight transmission mechanism

### B. Inflation Reacceleration
| Metric | Value | Period |
|--------|-------|--------|
| PCE inflation (annualized) | 4.5% | Q1 2026 |
| Core PCE (annualized) | 4.3% | Q1 2026 |
| PCE year-over-year | 3.5% | March 2026 |
| Core PCE year-over-year | 3.2% | March 2026 |
| CPI monthly change | +0.87% | March 2026 |

Breakeven inflation at ~2.50% remains well above the Fed's 2% target.

### C. Trade Policy / Tariff Impact
- **Feb 20, 2026:** Supreme Court struck down most IEEPA tariffs as unconstitutional; administration immediately imposed replacement tariffs under new legal authority
- **Liberation Day tariffs** (April 2, 2025): 189,600 blue-collar jobs lost in 10 months; manufacturing contracted for 8 consecutive months
- **100% Chinese EV tariff** remains in place with bipartisan support
- Tariff refund portal: only ~20% of owed refunds claimed; >1/3 of claims rejected
- **US dollar depreciation of ~10%** (Jan 2025 to Apr 2026), the worst first-half losses since 1973, contributing to imported inflation

### D. Fiscal Concerns
- Federal debt: **>$39 trillion** (>100% of GDP)
- Annual interest payments: **>$1 trillion**
- Deficit: **$1.6 trillion**
- Elevated term premia partly reflect fiscal sustainability concerns

## 3. Federal Reserve Policy & Rate Expectations

### Current Stance
- **Fed funds target:** 3.50%–3.75% (effective rate: 3.64%)
- **On hold since January 2026** after three 25bp cuts in Q4 2025
- The April 28–29 FOMC meeting saw **4 dissenting votes** — the most since October 1992
- Chair Powell: *"Inflation's getting worse, not better"*

### Market Pricing (Fed Funds Futures)
Markets price in **essentially zero rate changes through July 2026** and at most **~1 cut (14 bps) by late 2026**:
- May–July 2026: 3.64% (no change)
- October 2026: 3.60% (~1 cut priced)
- November 2026: 3.62%

### Dot Plot and Forecaster Views
- **FOMC median dot (March 2026 SEP):** ~3.4% by year-end (implies ONE 25bp cut)
- **7 of 19 participants** expect no cuts at all in 2026
- **J.P. Morgan (Feroli):** Zero Fed rate cuts through all of 2026
- **Manifold Markets:** 31% probability of a rate **hike** in 2026; only 23% probability of 3+ cuts
- **Goldman Sachs:** 30% recession probability within 12 months

### Fed Leadership Transition
- **Kevin Warsh** (Trump's Fed Chair nominee, favors lower rates) confirmation vote expected week of May 11
- **Jerome Powell** will remain on the Board of Governors after chairmanship ends
- **Trump v. Cook (Fed governor firing case):** SCOTUS heard arguments in January 2026; ruling expected by end of June

## 4. Related Market Indicators

| Indicator | Value | As Of |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| 30Y mortgage (Freddie Mac) | 6.30% | Apr 30 |
| 30Y mortgage (MND) | 6.44% | May 6 |
| 30Y FHA mortgage | 5.92% | May 6 |
| BBB corporate yield | 5.32% | Apr 30 |
| BBB OAS | 1.02% (102 bps) | Apr 30 |
| Q1 2026 GDP | +2.0% annualized | Q1 2026 |
| Unemployment | 4.3% | March 2026 |
| Initial claims | 189,000 (lowest since 1969) | Recent |
| Sahm Rule indicator | 0.20 | Recent |

## 5. Key Upcoming Catalysts

| Date | Event | Market Significance |
|------|-------|---------------------|
| May 8 | April Nonfarm Payrolls | Major market mover |
| May 11 | Warsh Senate confirmation vote | Fed leadership transition |
| May 12 | April CPI release | Critical inflation data |
| May 13 | April PPI release | Producer price gauge |
| May 28 | Q1 GDP second estimate | BEA revision |
| June 16–17 | FOMC meeting | Next rate decision |
| Late June | Trump v. Cook SCOTUS ruling | Fed independence implications |

---
